Help: Emulation: ANSI: Extended ASCII char set. VT100: ASCII char set. VT100Plus: Extends VT100 to support color, function keys, etc. VT-UTF8: Uses UTF8 encoding to map Unicode chars onto 1 or more bytes.
Title: Terminal Type

Help: Selects serial port transmission speed. The speed must be matched on the other side. Long or noisy lines may require lower speeds.
Title: Bits per second

Help: Data Bits
Title: Data Bits

Help: A parity bit can be sent with the data bits to detect some transmission errors. Even: parity bit is 0 if the num of 1's in the data bits is even. Odd: parity bit is 0 if num of 1's in the data bits is odd.  Mark: parity bit is always 1. Space: Parity bit is always 0. Mark and Space Parity do not allow for error detection. They can be used as an additional data bit.
Title: Parity

Help: Stop bits indicate the end of a serial data packet. (A start bit indicates the beginning). The standard setting is 1 stop bit. Communication with slow devices may require more than 1 stop bit.
Title: Stop Bits

Help: Flow control can prevent data loss from buffer overflow. When sending data, if the receiving buffers are full, a 'stop' signal can be sent to stop the data flow. Once the buffers are empty, a 'start' signal can be sent to re-start the flow. Hardware flow control uses two wires to send start/stop signals.
Title: Flow Control

Help: Defines number of Retry Attempts software will take to retrain the link if previous training attempt was unsuccessful.
Title: Link Training Retry

Help: Defines number of Microseconds software will wait before polling 'Link Training' bit in Link Status register. Value range from 10 to 10000 uS.
Title: Link Training Timeout (uS)

Help: In order to save power, software will disable unpopulated PCI Express links, if this option set to 'Disable Link'.
Title: Unpopulated Links

Help: In device Functions that support Completion Timeout programmability, allows system software to modify the Completion Timeout value. 'Default' 50us to 50ms. If 'Shorter' is selected, software will use shorter timeout ranges supported by hardware. If 'Longer' is selected, software will use longer timeout ranges.
Title: Completion Timeout

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', the Downstream Port disables its traditional Device Number field being 0 enforcement when turning a Type1 Configuration Request into a Type0 Configuration Request, permitting access to Extended Functions in an ARI Device immediately below the Port. Default value: Disabled
Title: ARI Forwarding

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', this function initiates AtomicOp Requests only if Bus Master Enable bit is in the Command Register Set.
Title: AtomicOp Requester Enable

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', outbound AtomicOp Requests via Egress Ports will be blocked.
Title: AtomicOp Egress Blocking

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', this permits setting the number of ID-Based Ordering (IDO) bit (Attribute[2]) requests to be initiated.
Title: IDO Request Enable

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', this permits setting the number of ID-Based Ordering (IDO) bit (Attribute[2]) requests to be initiated.
Title: IDO Completion Enable

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', this enables the Latency Tolerance Reporting (LTR) Mechanism.

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Enabled', this function will block forwarding of TLPs containing End-End TLP Prefixes.
Title: End-End TLP Prefix Blocking

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Force to X.X GT/s' for Downstream Ports, this sets an upper limit on Link operational speed by restricting the values advertised by the Upstream component in its training sequences. When 'Auto' is selected HW initialized data will be used.
Title: Target Link Speed

Help: signal for power management of Link clock in accordance to protocol defined in appropriate form factor specification.

Help: If supported by hardware and set to 'Disabled', this will disable the hardware's ability to change link speed except speed rate reduction for the purpose of correcting unstable link operation.

Help: Enter the value Between 1 to 30 min for OS Boot Watchdog Timer Expiration. Not available if OS Boot Watchdog Timer is disabled.
Title: OS Wtd Timer Timeout

Help: Configure how the system should respond if the OS Boot Watchdog Timer expires. Not available if OS Boot Watchdog Timer is disabled.
Title: OS Wtd Timer Policy

Help: Select the KSC interface state after POST end. If [Enabled] is selected, the BMC will remain KCS interface after POST stage. If [Disabled] is selected, the BMC will disable KCS interface after POST stage

Help: Select the power state after a power failure. If [Power Off] is selected, the power will remain off when the power recovers. If [Power On] is selected, the system will start to boot up when the power recovers.
Title: Restore AC Power Loss
